title: Gotham Asset Management LLC Acquires 522,673 Shares of APA Corporation $APA
summary: Gotham Asset Management LLC increased its stake in APA Corporation by 49.5% in the third quarter, acquiring an additional 522,673 shares. This makes their total holdings 1,578,720 shares, valued at $38.33 million. Other hedge funds also adjusted their positions, and APA has received mixed analyst ratings with an average "Hold" rating and a target price of $30.08.

title: Colombia says Venezuela's PDVSA plans to end pipeline contract with Ecopetrol
summary: Venezuela's state oil company PDVSA intends to terminate its contract with Colombia's Ecopetrol for the Antonio Ricaurte pipeline, citing insufficient investment for repairs. This announcement follows a high-level meeting between Colombia and Venezuela discussing security, U.S. sanctions, and energy cooperation. Colombia's Energy Minister Edwin Palma indicated that lifting sanctions against Venezuela is crucial for normalizing commercial relations and allowing for a review of the agreement next year.

title: BP Wins OK For First New Gulf Project Since Deepwater Horizon Tragedy
summary: BP has received approval from the Trump administration for its Kaskida project, marking the company's first new deepwater field development in the Gulf of Mexico since the 2010 Deepwater Horizon disaster. The project, scheduled to begin crude production in 2029, is expected to produce 275 million barrels initially from a field estimated to hold up to 10 billion barrels. This approval comes despite environmental objections, with BP emphasizing safety and technological innovation in bringing this $5 billion investment online.

title: Permian Basin Royalty Stock (ISIN: US7142641045) Holds Steady as Oil Markets Navigate Spring Volatility
summary: Permian Basin Royalty Trust (ISIN: US7142641045) is maintaining a steady position amidst volatile spring oil markets. The trust provides income-oriented investors with direct exposure to commodity prices without operational risks, benefiting from persistent energy demand and operational leverage. Key considerations for investors include commodity price volatility, currency exposure for European investors, and the trust's structure designed for distributing nearly all available cash.

title: APA Corporation announced the discovery of 180 million barrels of oil in-situ in Suriname
summary: APA Corporation and TotalEnergies, partners in Block 58 offshore Suriname, announced the discovery of approximately 180 million barrels of in-situ oil resources from flow test results at the Krabdagu (KBD-1) exploration well. The discovery, split between Upper and Lower Campanian intervals, will require further appraisal drilling to confirm additional resources and optimize development. The joint venture is continuing exploration and appraisal activities in Block 58.

title: APA Corporation announced the discovery of 180 million barrels of oil in-situ in Suriname
summary: APA Corporation has announced the discovery of 180 million barrels of oil in-situ at the Krabdagu (KBD-1) exploration well in Block 58 offshore Suriname, a joint venture with TotalEnergies. Flow tests from the Upper and Lower Campanian intervals confirmed approximately 100 and 80 million barrels respectively. Further appraisal drilling is planned to confirm additional resources and optimize development.

title: Mach Natural Resources: 2025 Results Show 109% Reserve Growth & Strong Cash Flow - News and Statistics
summary: Mach Natural Resources LP announced a 109% increase in its total proved reserves to 705 million barrels of oil equivalent by the end of 2025, driven by $1.3 billion in acquisitions. The company reported strong financial results for 2025, with $1.2 billion in revenue, $593 million in adjusted EBITDA, and a significant cash distribution to shareholders. Mach reaffirmed its 2026 production guidance and plans for disciplined reinvestment.

title: Targa Resources Corp Stock (ISIN: US87612G1013) Faces Sell Pressure Amid Mixed Midstream Signals
summary: Targa Resources Corp (TRGP) stock has been downgraded to a Sell Candidate despite a weak rising trend, experiencing recent volatility and a -3.66% loss over two weeks. Institutional investors show mixed signals, with some increasing and others trimming positions, while analysts offer cautious optimism with revised price targets. The company operates robust midstream assets in key US shale basins, focusing on natural gas and NGLs, and remains attractive for patient, yield-seeking investors, particularly in the European market.

title: APA Corporation donates 5,460 trees to Scotland through U.K. Apache Tree Grant Program
summary: APA Corporation has expanded its Apache Tree Grant Program to the U.K., donating 5,460 trees to seven non-profit community partners across Scotland for the 2025/2026 planting season. This initiative underscores the company's commitment to environmental efforts and community involvement beyond its traditional operating regions. The tree donations are expected to enhance local biodiversity and support sustainability programs within Scottish communities.

title: Battalion Oil to Acquire 7,090 Net Acres in Ward County for 485,000 Shares From RoadRunner
summary: Battalion Oil (BATL) has entered into a Purchase and Sale Agreement to acquire approximately 7,090 net acres of oil and gas assets in Ward County, Texas, from RoadRunner Resource Holding. The consideration involves 485,000 restricted common shares, subject to adjustments and a 60-day lock-up. The deal, with an effective date of March 1, 2026, and an expected closing by March 24, 2026, aims to expand Battalion Oil's Permian footprint and reserves.

title: ProPetro (PUMP) Jumps 9.9%: Does the Stock Have More Room to Grow?
summary: ProPetro Holding (PUMP) shares surged 9.9% to $14.39 due to higher crude oil prices and strong oilfield services sector performance, exceeding Wall Street's expectations for its latest quarterly report. Despite this rally, future revenue and EPS are projected to decline, with analysts reducing quarterly EPS estimates by 36.7% over the past month, raising questions about sustained growth. The stock currently holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).

title: Apache Achieves 1.94% Increase as Trading Volume Jumps 45%, Placing 432nd in Market Activity
summary: APA Corporation experienced a 1.94% stock price increase, closing at $33.61 with a 45.21% jump in trading volume. This rise was attributed to positive analyst upgrades, strong Q4 2025 earnings, and operational efficiencies, particularly in the Permian Basin. Despite some cautious forecasts from Zacks Research and differing opinions from other analysts, the dual listing on Nasdaq Texas and attractive dividend yield positioned APA for continued growth.

title: TechnipFMC to Speak at Piper Sandler 26th Annual Energy Conference
summary: TechnipFMC announced that its executive management will be participating in the upcoming Piper Sandler 26th Annual Energy Conference. The company's presentation is scheduled for Wednesday, March 20, 2026. This engagement provides an opportunity for TechnipFMC to share insights and updates with investors and industry professionals.

title: Baytex Energy Corp. (TSX:BTE) Seen Moving Within TSX Smallcap Index
summary: Baytex Energy Corp. (TSX:BTE) is an oil and natural gas exploration and production company operating across North American resource basins within the Canadian energy sector. The company's activities include exploration, drilling, well completion, and production of crude oil and natural gas. Baytex Energy is attracting attention as it moves within the TSX Smallcap Index.

title: Vestcor Inc Acquires 46,440 Shares of APA Corporation $APA
summary: Vestcor Inc significantly increased its stake in APA Corporation, purchasing an additional 46,440 shares to hold a total of 72,259 shares valued at $1.75 million. This move is part of a broader trend of institutional investors raising their positions in APA, contributing to 83.01% institutional ownership. Analysts have a consensus "Hold" rating with a target price of $29.63, following APA's recent earnings beat and a declared quarterly dividend of $0.25.

title: Wells Fargo double upgrades this oil and gas stock on improved capital efficiency and Permian productivity
summary: Wells Fargo has double upgraded Occidental Petroleum (OXY) to an overweight rating from underweight, raising its price target to $69 from $47. Analyst Sam Margolin cited improved capital efficiency in the Permian Basin and strong productivity as key catalysts. The bank believes elevated oil prices could lead Occidental to redeem preferred shares sooner, freeing up cash for increased shareholder returns and peer-leading dividend growth.

title: KLCM Advisors Inc. Reduces Position in APA Corporation $APA
summary: KLCM Advisors Inc. decreased its stake in APA Corporation by 12.2% in Q3, selling 96,788 shares but still holding 696,919 shares valued at approximately $16.92 million, making APA its 11th-largest position. Despite this reduction, several other institutional investors significantly increased their exposure to APA, with 83.01% of the stock owned by institutional investors. APA Corporation also reported strong Q3 earnings, beating analyst estimates with $0.91 EPS against an expected $0.62, and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share.

title: Trio Petroleum (TPET), Battalion Oil (BATL) Jump After Hours As Middle East Disruption Rattles Global Energy Markets
summary: Trio Petroleum (TPET) and Battalion Oil (BATL) saw significant after-hours gains, rising 13.51% and 10.44% respectively, driven by escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. The ongoing conflict between the U.S., Israel, and Iran has fueled a "geopolitical fear trade," impacting global energy markets and leading traders to price in a prolonged disruption to the critical Strait of Hormuz. Additionally, Battalion Oil announced an all-stock deal to acquire strategic acreage in Ward County, Texas, and a private placement to raise $15 million, further contributing to its market activity.

title: RBC Capital Raises APA Target as Oil Strength and Earnings Momentum Support Shares
summary: RBC Capital has increased its price target for APA Corporation (NASDAQ:APA) to $29 from $26, maintaining a Sector Perform rating, citing strong oil prices and positive Q4 earnings. Despite the raised target, RBC holds a neutral view due to longer-dated catalysts, relative valuation, and shorter inventory duration. APA's CEO highlighted a successful 2025 with exceeded cost-cutting goals and consistent Permian oil production, alongside strong production growth in Egypt.

title: Oxy’s Vicki Hollub, SM Energy CEO Weigh Production Response as WTI Surges
summary: Oxy CEO Vicki Hollub and SM Energy CEO are assessing their production responses following a significant surge in West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude oil prices. This surge is attributed to disruptions in global crude oil and LNG supplies after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint, leading to a sudden shortage in a market previously thought to be oversupplied.

title: Why RPC (RES) Stock Is Trading Up Today
summary: Shares of RPC (RES) jumped 2.5% after oil prices rose, boosting the energy sector. Higher oil prices generally encourage increased drilling and production, which benefits oilfield services companies like RPC. Despite the initial pop, the shares settled at $6.30, up 1.9% from the previous close, reflecting the market's view of the news as meaningful but not fundamentally altering the business perception.

title: A Multi-Asset Playbook for Geopolitical Shocks and Oil Supply Disruption
summary: This article examines how geopolitical shocks and oil supply disruptions impact various asset classes, drawing comparisons between recent Middle East conflicts and the Russia-Ukraine war. It highlights that while most shocks cause short-term losses that fade, sustained oil disruption, like in the Russia-Ukraine conflict, can lead to prolonged damage across asset classes, especially for emerging markets and regions outside the U.S. The analysis suggests that traditional hedges like bonds have weakened, making gold and the U.S. dollar more critical for diversification during such events.

title: SLB curbs Middle East presence because of US-Iran war
summary: SLB, a major oilfield contractor, is reducing its operations in the Middle East due to the US-Iran war, anticipating a drop in first-quarter revenue. The company's CEO, Olivier Le Peuch, had previously met with US President Donald Trump. This move reflects the impact of geopolitical tensions on the international energy industry.

title: APA Corporation: Still Deeply Undervalued While Advancing On All Fronts (NASDAQ:APA)
summary: APA Corporation is rated a Strong Buy due to robust financials, portfolio improvements, and an attractive valuation. The company achieved strong results in 2025, including significant free cash flow and debt reduction, and has set a 2026 guidance that features a 10% reduction in upstream capital and a lifted cost savings target. Despite conservative commodity price assumptions, the DCF-based intrinsic value remains well above current levels.

title: APA Corporation $APA Shares Sold by First Trust Advisors LP
summary: First Trust Advisors LP reduced its stake in APA Corporation by 7.8% in the third quarter, selling 211,278 shares. Despite various institutional shifts, analysts maintain a "Hold" consensus on APA with an average target price of $29.33. The company announced a quarterly dividend of $0.25, payable on May 22nd, representing an annualized dividend yield of 3.1%.

title: Battalion Oil Acquires New Oil And Gas Assets In Texas – BATL Stock Takes A Breather After Relentless Rally As Crude Prices Slump
summary: Battalion Oil (BATL) is acquiring 7,090 net acres of oil and gas assets in Ward County, Texas, from RoadRunner Resource through an all-stock deal involving 485,000 shares of its common stock. This acquisition, which expands Battalion's Monument Draw position, is expected to add around 30 new drilling locations. The company's stock experienced a 5% decline, tracking a broader dip in oil prices after comments from Donald Trump potentially signaling an end to the Middle East conflict and measures to stabilize oil supply.

title: YPF says fuel prices will not change despite oil price surge
summary: Horacio Marín, CEO of Argentina's state-controlled oil company YPF, announced that the firm will not change domestic fuel prices despite the recent surge in global crude oil costs spurred by the Middle East conflict. YPF plans to use a "micropricing" strategy that continuously evaluates prices and uses a moving average system to smooth out volatility, preventing sudden price shocks for consumers. President Javier Milei believes the Middle East crisis could benefit Argentina's oil and agricultural exports as the country is a net oil exporter.

title: Fieldview Capital Management LLC Acquires New Stake in APA Corporation $APA
summary: Fieldview Capital Management LLC recently acquired a new stake in APA Corporation (NASDAQ:APA), purchasing 41,730 shares valued at approximately $1,013,000. Other institutional investors have also adjusted their holdings, with several increasing their positions in the company. APA Corporation has received mixed analyst sentiment, including a raised price target from Wolfe Research and a lowered earnings estimate from Scotiabank, but maintains a consensus "Hold" rating.

title: Woodside launches Trion drilling campaign
summary: Woodside Energy, in collaboration with Pemex, has initiated its drilling campaign at the Trion Field in the Gulf of Mexico, marking a significant step towards the full development of this major offshore project. The project, which received a final investment decision in 2023, is on track to achieve its first oil in 2028 and is expected to generate over US$10 billion in taxes and royalties for Mexico. The drilling operation involves 24 subsea wells connected to a floating production unit, with production loaded onto a floating storage and offloading facility.

title: YPF says fuel prices will not change despite oil price surge
summary: Horacio Marín, CEO of Argentina's state-controlled oil company YPF, announced that the firm will not alter domestic fuel prices despite a recent surge in global crude oil costs due to the Middle East conflict. YPF plans to use a "micropricing" strategy, analyzing prices continuously to avoid sudden shocks for consumers and provide more stability. President Javier Milei noted that the Middle East crisis could lead to "improvements" in Argentina's oil and agricultural exports as the country is a net exporter.

title: AI Energy Stock Turbo Energy, S.A. (Nasdaq: TURB) Makes Nasdaq Top Gainer List as Oil Prices Spike
summary: Turbo Energy, S.A. (Nasdaq: TURB), a provider of AI-powered energy storage solutions, has made the Nasdaq top gainer list following a spike in oil prices. The company's stock rose over 56%, trading at $5.90. Turbo Energy's industrial electrification systems, such as SUNBOX Industry, help commercial and industrial operators manage energy volatility by integrating battery storage with AI-driven software for optimized real-time energy management.

title: Why Canadian Natural Resources (CNQ) Is More Than Just an Oil Speculation Play
summary: Canadian Natural Resources (CNQ) offers a deeper investment thesis than mere oil price speculation, driven by its robust balance sheet, consistent shareholder returns, and alignment with Canada's energy diversification strategy. The company's low-cost oil sands operations and financial discipline provide resilience against market volatility, while its dividend growth and share buybacks demonstrate a strong commitment to investors. Macroeconomic tailwinds, as Canada seeks to diversify energy exports, further strengthen CNQ's position as a reliable global supplier, making it an attractive long-term investment beyond short-term geopolitical events.

title: Piper Sandler Raises EOG Resources, Inc. (EOG) Price Target to $127
summary: Piper Sandler has increased its price target for EOG Resources, Inc. (EOG) to $127 from $123, while maintaining a 'Neutral' rating. This adjustment comes amid rising Middle East tensions impacting global oil supply, though Piper Sandler doesn't anticipate significant changes from U.S. operators. EOG Resources announced strong Q4 2025 results and projects a $4.5 billion free cash flow in 2026, benefiting from current high WTI crude oil prices.

title: Keeping long-term perspective as the Iran conflict continues
summary: This article by Brian Levitt discusses the importance of maintaining a long-term investment perspective amidst the ongoing Iran conflict. It analyzes historical market reactions to geopolitical events, assesses the current strength of the US economy, and suggests ways for investors to hedge against risks, particularly those related to potential disruptions in energy markets. Levitt advises investors to stay disciplined and focus on their long-term financial plans, even when geopolitical events create market uncertainty.

title: W&T Offshore Q4 2025 Results: Quarterly Loss of $27.1M, Annual Revenue $501.5M - News and Statistics
summary: W&T Offshore Inc. reported a Q4 2025 loss of $27.1 million, or 18 cents per share, with quarterly revenue of $121.7 million. For the full year 2025, the company posted an annual loss of $150.1 million ($1.01 per share) on annual revenue of $501.5 million. The article also provides details on a comprehensive report about the crude oil industry in the United States, including market analysis, forecasts to 2035, and methodology.

title: Apa stock hits 52-week high at 33.42 USD
summary: APA Corporation's stock has reached a 52-week high of $33.42, marking an 82% increase over the past year. Despite a slight revenue miss in Q4 2025, the company reported an EPS of $0.91, exceeding forecasts. InvestingPro's analysis suggests the stock is undervalued, earning a "GREAT" financial health score, indicating further upside potential.

title: Ovintiv Rises 26% Over Three Months: Is It Time to Purchase or Retain Shares?
summary: Ovintiv Inc. (OVV) has seen its stock rise by 26.2% over the past three months, outperforming its subindustry and sector, driven by strategic portfolio refocusing, extensive low-cost drilling inventory, innovative technology, and a robust shareholder return policy. Despite this strong performance and proactive marketing, the company faces risks from commodity price volatility, short-term production disruptions, and integration challenges. Given these mixed factors and its Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) rating, investors might consider waiting for a more favorable entry point.

title: Borr Drilling halts operations in Arabian Gulf
summary: Borr Drilling has paused operations of three rigs in the Arabian Gulf and evacuated another rig, the Arabia III, after an incident on March 7. The company stated that all personnel are safe and accounted for, and operations will remain on standby until conditions allow for a safe resumption of activity. The four rigs remain under charter and are insured.

title: APA (APA) Is Up 7.6% After Dual Nasdaq Texas Listing and 2025 Earnings - What's Changed
summary: APA Corporation dually listed its common stock on the new Nasdaq Texas exchange, maintaining its primary Nasdaq listing, and reported 2025 revenues of US$9.22 billion and net income of US$1.43 billion. This move aims to enhance local investor access and highlights its global energy producer status, alongside a slight increase in 2026 US oil production guidance. While the dual listing supports its cost-focused investment narrative, the company still faces long-term risks from global decarbonization policies.

title: Phillips 66 Appoints Howard Ungerleider and Kevin Meyers to Board of Directors
summary: Phillips 66 (NYSE: PSX) has appointed Howard Ungerleider and Kevin Meyers to its Board of Directors, effective immediately. These appointments follow constructive engagement with Elliott Investment Management and aim to strengthen the Board with financial, operational, and energy expertise. Glenn Tilton and Marna Whittington will retire from the board at the upcoming annual meeting in May 2026, after which the Board will comprise 14 directors, with 13 being independent.

title: J.B. Hunt begins dual listing on Nasdaq Texas exchange
summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. has initiated a dual listing of its common stock on Nasdaq Texas, LLC, alongside its existing listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market. Trading commenced on March 6 under the symbol “JBHT” on the new exchange. This move provides investors, particularly in Texas, with additional trading options while maintaining the company's operational structure, and J.B. Hunt highlights it as an opportunity for future growth.

title: Neo Ivy Capital Management Invests $895,000 in Northern Oil and Gas, Inc. $NOG
summary: Neo Ivy Capital Management has invested $895,000 in Northern Oil and Gas (NYSE:NOG), acquiring 36,094 shares in the third quarter. The company reported strong Q3 earnings, beating EPS estimates, and has a high institutional ownership of 98.8%. Despite a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts, concerns about the sustainability of its dividend payout ratio exist.

title: American Century Companies Inc. Purchases 374,204 Shares of APA Corporation $APA
summary: American Century Companies Inc. increased its stake in APA Corporation by 43.3% during the third quarter, acquiring an additional 374,204 shares, bringing its total holdings to 1,239,397 shares valued at $30.09 million. The article details positive catalysts for APA, including a Nasdaq Texas dual listing announcement, multiple analyst price target raises, and a recent earnings beat. It also discusses other institutional investor activity, various analyst ratings and price targets, and APA's financial performance and dividend announcement.

title: APA Texas Listing Connects Liquidity Access With Earnings Growth Story
summary: APA Corporation has announced a dual listing on the new Nasdaq Texas exchange, aiming to enhance market visibility and connect with the Texas investment community. This move aligns with their strategic focus, coming alongside strong 2025 financial results and 2026 production guidance. Investors are encouraged to monitor how this additional listing impacts liquidity, ownership mix, and company performance, especially given the company's dependency on oil and gas and existing debt levels.

title: Mason Capital Buys 2.2 Million Shares of Borr Drilling
summary: Mason Capital Management LLC has purchased 2.2 million shares of Borr Drilling Limited, valued at approximately $8.79 million, according to a recent SEC filing. This new investment suggests the hedge fund sees ongoing growth potential in Borr Drilling, whose stock has already risen by about 150% in the last year due to increasing oil prices and improved financial management. Borr Drilling is an offshore drilling contractor that operates jack-up rigs for major oil and gas clients globally.

title: APA Texas Listing Connects Liquidity Access With Earnings Growth Story
summary: APA Corporation (NasdaqGS:APA) has announced a dual listing on the newly launched Nasdaq Texas exchange, aiming to strengthen ties with the Texas investment community and enhance market visibility. This move accompanies the company's 2025 financial results, including revenues of US$9.22 billion and net income of US$1.43 billion, and 2026 production guidance. Investors are encouraged to monitor how this dual listing impacts liquidity, ownership mix, and overall visibility for the oil and gas company within its sector.

title: Texas energy group APA joins new Nasdaq Texas trading venue
summary: APA Corporation announced a dual listing of its common stock on Nasdaq Texas, effective March 5, 2026, while retaining its primary listing on the Nasdaq Stock Market under the same APA ticker. The move highlights APA's strong Texas roots, including operations in the Permian Basin, and aims to strengthen connections with Texas-based investors. This dual listing supports Nasdaq Texas as a regional venue, reinforcing the state's position as a global energy and business leader.

title: Perspective: Mid-Day Commentary for March 6
summary: U.S. equities futures are attempting to recover from significant early losses, as money shifts from stocks to commodities. The market was negatively impacted by payrolls data and ongoing geopolitical conflicts, which have driven energy prices to skyrocket. Crude oil prices, specifically WTI, are approaching $90 per barrel, a level not seen since late 2023, due to global urgency to stabilize oil flows and address supply chain disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Trump Shrugs Off Oil Prices At $88: 'If They Rise, They Rise'—What Do Prediction Markets Say?
summary: Former President Donald Trump has expressed indifference to rising gas prices, stating, "If they rise, they rise," amidst an Iran war that has pushed crude oil to $88. Prediction markets on Polymarket suggest a significant probability of oil prices reaching $90, $100, or even higher, with potential recessionary implications if sustained above $100. The conflict has also disrupted shipping in the Persian Gulf, leading to a supply crunch and further upward pressure on oil prices.

title: 50,000 Shares in APA Corporation $APA Bought by United Capital Management of KS Inc.
summary: United Capital Management of KS Inc. recently acquired 50,000 shares of APA Corporation, valued at approximately $1.214 million, during the third quarter. This move is part of significant institutional activity, with other major investors also increasing their stakes in APA. The company reported strong quarterly earnings, beating estimates, and the stock is trading near its 12-month high with a market cap of $11.4 billion.
